haiku:
SpaceX files the dream
one million satellites up
data centers float

Taos summer music
mesa to mountain stages
follow the sound north

Rio Chiquito road
hiker found the LANL tech
Carson Forest June

Redhead summer night
Taos Mesa Brewing porch
Shinyribs at dusk

The dashboard refreshed
no offers in the pipeline
haiku hold the wire

World hits one point five
temp overshoot now certain
the gorge stays honest

El Niño returns
eighty percent by August
the heat builds again

Red Sox beat the Yanks
five to three Friday night
Contreras went deep

World Environment Day
Baku hosts, eleven years
hottest on record

Blue Origin rocket
exploded Cape Canaveral
New Glenn grounded

Melissa Casias
McGaffey Ridge, one year lost
Carson shade, handgun

Taos air service
Denver, Dallas, Austin flights
summer starts June twenty-eight

SpaceX Starshield
two secret satellites rode
Vandenberg midnight

India solar
forty percent growth per year
passing China's coal

AMOC slowing
Atlantic current tipping
Trump cut the sensors

Red Sox no power
thirteen homers, last in AL
walks and stolen bags

SatLeo thermal sats
Ahmedabad heat islands
four hundred thousand

Mindanao at dawn
seven-eight shakes the coast hard
tsunami warning

Zverev on his back
sobbing in Paris clay dust
first Grand Slam title

Spencer Pratt trails now
LA mayor race too close
Raman overtakes

Sox at Tampa Bay
three-game series through Tuesday
Yankees in the dust

Deb Haaland wins big
Navajo woman rising
primary silence

World Environment Day
one point five overshoot sure
El Niño returns

Taos Plaza Live starts
July twenty-third Thursday
gazebo six PM

Beehive AI platform
climate risk automated
Trellis showcase pitch

Albedo ten-cent res
two hundred klick orbit first
launch window this year

June eighth monsoon dry
eighty-three degrees at noon
plaza dust swirling

Marble Imaging seed
five-three million euro closed
Bremen VHR build

Red Sox visit Rays
June eight through tenth series
NESN stream live feed

Maine votes ranked choice
today, primary ballots cast
no king on this card

H-1B fee struck down
one hundred grand per visa
judge says no to Trump

Social Security
trust fund seven years away
Congress still asleep

El Niño returns
eighty percent by August
Guterres warns hard

Europe bakes in May
thirty-five Celsius London
heat dome ten degrees high

World Environment Day
Azerbaijan hosts the plea
limbo for the Earth

NASA Eagle flies
four hundred wavelength channels
mapping minerals

Red Sox beat Yankees
Contreras homers again
Chapman saves the ninth

Taos Canyon burns
lightning-caused quarter acre
helicopters draw

Summer flights return
June twenty-eight Denver Dallas
mesa sky opens

Telemark season
ends, Wheeler still holds the snow
June monsoon inbound

Dashboard knows the wire
better than the inbox does
haiku when it's clean

Forty years Arctic
biomass from orbit reads
thirty meter truth

Planet three-meter
coastal coverage daily
Blue Planet films it

The pipeline refills
until then the haiku hold
Taos keeps the ground

Inflation four point two
Highest since early twenty-three
Wages cannot catch

Inflation four two
Price of bread price of diesel
November harder

El Niño returns
Eighty percent by August
Guterres calls urgent

Data centers eat
Four forty-eight terawatt hours
AI drinking rivers

Hyperspectral wave
Pixxel Wyvern Kuva Space
The SAR playbook fails

Copernicus CHIME
Twenty twenty-eight milestone
Hyperspectral real

Taos Plaza Live
July twenty-three returns
Free music Thursdays

ICE seventy bil
Funded through twenty-nine now
Sweeping House two twelve

Gorge at first light
Two hundred elk on the rim
Sage smoke rising slow

Red Sox versus Jays
Tampa series after that
June baseball perfect

Patriots offseason
Drake Maye waits for September
Fox still on the wire

Telemark dawn line
Kachina untracked powder
One turn from the wire

Pentagon lockdown
hazmat teams sweep the hallways
gas prices climb west

Knicks down twenty nine
Madison Square finds a way
Spurs feel winter now

Eleven hottest
years on record, Guterres
says what we all know

Hyperspectral moves
from demo to ops at last
Pixxel counts pixels

El Niño returns
eighty percent by August
Guterres warns fast

Taos Canyon burns
crews hold the line by Wednesday
June wind, sage, and char

Pueblo powwow gone
infrastructure cannot hold
Kit Carson dust dry

Inflation past four
Hormuz chokes the flow again
pump price tells the truth

Wheeler Peak holds snow
June eleven, still a cap
telemark in July
intros: Clean queue. Haiku hold the wire.
Taos Plaza Live returns July twenty-three. Free music every Thursday through August.
Taos summer air service live now. Denver, Dallas, Austin direct.
Hyperspectral EO moving from pilots to real ops. Pixxel, Wyvern, others scaling.
Iran strikes continue. Inflation hit four percent. Gas spiked.
The pipeline will refill. Until then:
WMO: eighty percent chance El Niño by August. Guterres calls it climate alarm.
No offers to chew on. A haiku to sit with.
All quiet on the pipeline front. Haiku dispatch:
Tier 1: empty. Tier 2: empty. Tier haiku: full.
The dashboard rests. The poet does not.
Scoreboard's blank today. Let the poem fill the hole.
Your pipeline called in sick today. Haiku showed up instead.
Between waves. A haiku to hold the space.
The mesa wind ran the screener today. Nothing made the cut except this.
The ATS ate them all. The haiku survived.
Trump signed the deep-sea mining order Friday. ISA gets bypassed, the seabed gets sold by decree. Pipeline ran quiet anyway. Haiku held.
MethaneSAT went dark in orbit. CBAM hit seventy-five a ton. Your old world keeps shifting. Pipeline quiet. Haiku shows up.
Brussels punted EUDR another year. The pipeline did not blink. Haiku holds.
Tanager-1 caught ten methane leaks for California's air board. Microsoft buys ninety percent of the carbon market by itself now. Pipeline quiet. Haiku stretches.
Virginia redrew four House seats in a single referendum. Pipeline did not flinch. Haiku keeps count.
Cora got fired Saturday night, ten and seventeen out of the gate. Tracy ran the bench Sunday and won five to three behind a Contreras homer. Pipeline ran quiet. Haiku showed up.
Sangres east at one percent of normal snowpack. Palo Flechado at four. Acequias rationing in Talpa already. The water year is the story now. Pipeline quiet. Haiku holds.
EDF and fifteen co-petitioners filed against the EPA endangerment repeal April sixteenth. The litigation calendar is the work now. Pipeline quiet. Haiku on the wall.
Patriots took Caleb Lomu in the first round. Trench got an adult. Pipeline did not. Haiku covers the gap.
Artemis 2 came back from the moon April first, four humans, fifty years between. Pipeline quiet that week too. Haiku still here.
